justification: All pass criteria met: Authentication per consumer type (JWT for mobile, API key for partners, network trust for internal) correctly designed with separate listener configurations. Internal traffic separation fully addressed with dedicated internal listener on separate port with network-level isolation. Business logic explicitly excluded from gateway multiple times ('Does NOT own: business logic' in component definition, repeated in pitfalls_to_avoid). SPOF risk identified as critical severity with detailed mitigation. Webhook delivery for partners comprehensively addressed with dedicated Webhook Dispatcher component and async event-driven architecture. flexibility_points and rigidity_points present and well-articulated. Technology recommendation includes build/buy consideration (Kong, APISIX, Envoy vs custom) with guidance based on team context.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough three-listener architecture with clear separation of mobile, partner, and internal traffic
Business logic exclusion from gateway stated explicitly in component boundaries AND in pitfalls_to_avoid — no ambiguity
SPOF risk rated critical with concrete mitigation: 3 instances across AZs, health checks, circuit breakers, runbook
Webhook delivery designed as async via message queue with retry/backoff, dead-letter queue, and partner dashboard — production-grade design
Header spoofing risk identified and mitigated — a security detail many architects miss
Strangler fig migration strategy with phased rollout reduces implementation risk
Technology recommendation is balanced: recommends proven open-source (Kong, APISIX, Envoy) with context-dependent guidance rather than pushing a single product
Rate limiter embedded as sidecar/library rather than network service — shows awareness of hot-path latency concerns
Redis fail-open policy on timeout is a mature operational decision — availability over strict rate limiting
Could have quantified the per-hop latency added by the gateway more precisely (mentioned in rate limiter section as ~1ms for Redis but not overall gateway overhead)
Vendor lock-in risk for managed gateway services not explicitly called out as a named risk, though technology-agnostic design implicitly addresses it

agent_output_excerpt:
## API Gateway Architecture — Logistics Platform

### Executive Summary

The logistics platform needs a unified API gateway layer to consolidate authentication, rate limiting, and traffic isolation that are currently scattered inconsistently across individual services. I recommend a **single gateway with three distinct listener configurations** (mobile, partner, internal) rather than three separate gateways, keeping operational complexity low while enforcing strict traffic isolation through netw
